headline: Orang Asli Scholarship Access Rate Only 3.2% of Eligible Students
context: Despite dedicated scholarship programs, only 3.2% of eligible Orang Asli students receive higher education funding.

cards.big: Only 3.2% of SPM-completing Orang Asli students receive any tertiary scholarship
cards.sub: Bumiputera scholarship pools are dominated by Malay applicants from urban areas

cards.big: JAKOA education fund of RM28 million in 2025 serves 1,800 students total
cards.sub: Per-student allocation is RM15,500 insufficient for full degree sponsorship

cards.big: Orang Asli are constitutionally Bumiputera under Article 160 but practically marginalized in access
cards.sub: Legal status does not translate to proportional share of affirmative action benefits

cards.big: Canada's First Nations education funding was increased 47% after a 2016 tribunal ruling
cards.sub: Legal accountability forced government to address indigenous education underfunding

cards.big: Affirmative action that bypasses the most disadvantaged Bumiputera defeats its stated purpose
Should Orang Asli receive priority allocation within Bumiputera scholarship quotas
